cpu: AMD Ryzen 5 2400G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes: Would start to load, but screen would stay black and would lose mouse function. Had to hard reboot.
title: Dungeons & Dragons: Chronicles of Mystara

notes: PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC=30 %command% needed in launch options to get it to work perfectly. Otherwise it'll still work but not as well.
title: Dungeons & Dragons: Chronicles of Mystara

notes:
Basic keyboard controls work, as well as the usual gameplay modes and options with more than adequate performance.

However, a small handful of controllers including a PDP Rock Candy wired 360 pad, official XBox One pad via USB, and a Hori Fighting Commander in all three modes (PS4, PS3, and PC) were not detected at all by the game.   I have attempted with both controller configuration enabled by default, and forced off.
title: Dungeons & Dragons: Chronicles of Mystara

notes: The game initially launched but gave me a black screen, as other users reported, but looking closely to steam logs I figured out I had to install the "libnvidia-glvkspirv:i386" package. After this things worked fine.
title: Dungeons & Dragons: Chronicles of Mystara

notes:
This is now perfect out of the box.

PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC=30 %command% are NO LONGER needed in launch options.
title: Dungeons & Dragons: Chronicles of Mystara

notes:
Running game with default settings caused black screen for about 1 min then very slow choppy animations for capcom logo and commands were unresponsive.

Used PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command% and game ran perfectly after. Played for over an hour with absolutely no issues. Would have given game platinum rating if it wasnt for this minor tweak.
title: Dungeons & Dragons: Chronicles of Mystara

notes: Works out of the box, no tweaking needed, gamepad works out of the box as well
title: Dungeons & Dragons: Chronicles of Mystara
